title: Run your k6 test in Grafana Cloud

blocks.content: Authoring in Assistant is only half the value—the script has to land in **Grafana Cloud k6**, run at the scale you expect, and feed results back into Grafana dashboards. This milestone focuses on that handoff and what to watch on the first execution.

## Hand off and execute

To run the script Assistant produced, complete the following steps:

1. Copy the final JavaScript from Assistant (or download the attachment if you saved it that way).
2. In Grafana Cloud, open **Testing & synthetics** > **Performance** and either **create a new test** or open an existing one where you want this scenario to live.
3. Paste the script into the **k6 script editor**, then save. Resolve any editor validation messages before you schedule a run.
4. Start a **small trial run** (or your product’s equivalent quick run) with conservative VUs first, then scale up once checks and thresholds look right. Watch:
   - **HTTP status and check pass rates**—failures should match what you saw in Assistant checks.
   - **Threshold results**—this is your automated pass or fail gate.
   - **Endpoint-level latency**—confirm URL grouping produces readable series, not thousands of unique label values.
   - **Tracing hooks** (if present): in Grafana **Tempo**, confirm trace IDs or spans from k6 correlate with the run window you expect.
   - **Profiling hooks** (if present): in **Pyroscope**, confirm flame graphs or profiles show your service during the load window.
5. If the run fails for infrastructure reasons (timeouts, DNS, auth), capture the error, return to Assistant with the log snippet, and iterate. If the editor rejects the script, use the path cover **Troubleshooting** section and [k6 options](https://grafana.com/docs/k6/latest/using-k6/options/).

After a clean smoke, scale VUs or duration gradually. For long-term comparison of runs, refer to [Establish a performance baseline with k6](https://grafana.com/docs/learning-paths/establish-k6-baseline/).
In the final milestone, you wrap up with related docs and suggested follow-on paths.
